title: Xbox Accessibility Guidelines
creator: Microsoft
description: A platform-neutral set of detailed guidelines covering text, color, input, difficulty, audio, communication, motion, menus, and other player needs. Each guideline includes intent, implementation ideas, and testing questions.
useful: The testable criteria help teams turn a broad commitment to accessibility into reviewable design and QA work.
audience: Designers, engineers, producers, and testers evaluating accessibility throughout development.
learning: Find guidance for a specific feature or barrier
Translate player needs into implementation questions
Include accessibility criteria in design and test plans
